text:
If you're hoping for a dry stretch, it's close, just not quite here yet. The front's still clearing this morning, with lingering showers and a stray rumble of thunder possible through around midday, then things dry out for the afternoon under some partly sunny breaks. The forecasters are watching this closely, and honestly the models don't fully agree on how wet it gets, so I'd keep a rain jacket handy through lunchtime even though the bulk of the day should end up fine. High near 75, humidity easing as the day goes on. Out on the lake, south winds 15 to 20 knots this morning will make for a bumpy ride before backing off and swinging west tonight, so if you're headed out there, afternoon is your better window.

Thursday's the payoff: sunny, high near 79, and the start of a genuinely nice stretch heading into the weekend. If you can push outdoor plans to Thursday, do it.
packet:
OBSERVED (2026-07-22T11:10:00+00:00): Mostly Cloudy, 72F feels 72F, humidity 83%, wind S 9 mph
NWS FORECAST:
  Today: A chance of showers and thunderstorms between 7am and 11am, then a chance of showers and thunderstorms between 11am and noon, then a slight chance of showers and thunderstorms between noon and 2pm. Partly sunny, with a high near 75. Southwest wind 9 to 14 mph. Chance of precipitation is 30%. New rainfall amounts less than a tenth of an inch possible.
  Tonight: Partly cloudy, with a low around 56. West wind 6 to 13 mph.
  Thursday: Sunny, with a high near 79. West wind around 8 mph.
  Thursday Night: Mostly clear, with a low around 59. Southwest wind 3 to 7 mph.
AFD KEY MESSAGES (forecaster's reasoning, issued 2026-07-22T11:06:00+00:00):
  1. Lingering showers are expected today with isolated thunderstorms possible as a cold front moves through, then the weather pattern becomes drier for the latter half of the week.
  2. Quiet weather and increasing temperatures are expected this upcoming weekend before another system bring showers and thunderstorms to the region early next week.
AFD WHAT CHANGED: As of 527 AM EDT Wednesday... Have sent out an early morning update to adjust probability of precipitation and weather over the next few hours based on high resolution models that are performing well in comparison with the radar imagery and automated reports across the region. Today, we're thinking about 15-25% chance of precip in the St. Lawrence Valley, 50-80% chance of precip on the northern and western slopes of the Adirondacks and northern Greens, 5-30% chance of precip in the southern Champlain and Connecticut River valleys, and 30-50% elsewhere. Will be a mix of showers and embedded thunderstorms, drying out by the end of the day.
LAKE (broad waters):
  Today: South winds 15 to 20 knots, becoming west. Partly sunny. Scattered showers and thunderstorms in the morning, then scattered showers in the afternoon. Waves 1 to 2 feet.
  Tonight: West winds 15 to 20 knots, diminishing to 5 to 15 knots. Partly cloudy. Waves 1 to 2 feet.
  Thursday: Southwest winds 5 to 10 knots, becoming west. Sunny. Waves 1 foot or less.
  Thursday Night: West winds less than 10 knots, becoming south. Clear. Waves 1 foot or less.
LAKE GAGE: water 71F, level 95.82 ft (normal)
MODEL SPREAD (where forecasts diverge):
  2026-07-22: highs [GFS 79, Euro 78, ICON 74] spread 5F; precip chance [GFS 36%, Euro 100%, ICON 40%]
  2026-07-23: highs [GFS 78, Euro 77, ICON 78] spread 1F; precip chance [GFS 1%, Euro 10%, ICON 0%]
  2026-07-24: highs [GFS 81, Euro 80, ICON 81] spread 1F; precip chance [GFS 0%, Euro 0%, ICON 0%]
AIR: AQI 32 Good (OZONE) — On Wednesday, July 22nd, expect a chance for some low-moderate fine particulate matter pollution in Vermont as residual wildfire smoke continues to circulate in the northeast US and Canada.
SUN: rise 2026-07-22T05:29-04:00, set 2026-07-22T20:29-04:00, UV max 6.45
WEATHER UNDERGROUND / WEATHER.COM:
  high 76, low 55: Showers ending by midday. Highs in the mid 70s and lows in the mid 50s.
  high 80, low 58: More sun than clouds. Highs in the low 80s and lows in the upper 50s.
WCAX METEOROLOGIST: After the rain and front clears through the area, we are looking pretty nice to close the week and heading into next weekend. Sunshine returns Thursday as high temperatures for the day climb back into the mid and upper 70s. Sunshine lasts into Friday, Saturday and Sunday with highs warming into the low to mid-80s all the way through the upcoming weekend. There is a slight chance for a spotty shower or thunderstorm Sunday, but I suspect most stay dry.
